input.initialPrompt: Modernize this project to SDK-style format. Identify all legacy patterns and suggest how to simplify the project file.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to modernize the .csproj file from the old-style format to the new SDK-style format. Let me first examine the current project file and source files.

data.content:
Here's a summary of every legacy pattern removed and what replaced it:

| Legacy Pattern | What Changed |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, XML declaration | Replaced with `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| Manual `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Implicit via SDK |
| `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| `Configuration`/`Platform` defaults | Implicit SDK defaults |
| `ProjectGuid`, `OutputType` (Library), `RootNamespace`, `AssemblyName` | Removed — all match SDK defaults (name = folder/project name) |
| `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` | SDK defaults |
| Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks (output path, symbols, optimize, constants, warning level, error report) | All handled by SDK conventions |
| Explicit `<Reference>` for BCL assemblies (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) | Implicit for .NET Framework via SDK |
| Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK auto-globs all `*.cs` files |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>` (`Version`, `Description`, `Company`, etc.); file deleted |

The project went from **47 lines → 13 lines** with identical build semantics.
